headlines.headline: Five Killed in Russian Attacks on Southern Kherson Region
headlines.summary: Russian forces killed five civilians in three separate attacks on Ukraine southern Kherson region on Friday, as fighting continues despite ongoing ceasefire discussions.

headlines.headline: Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance as Midterm Pressures Mount
headlines.summary: Republican lawmakers are showing increased willingness to break with Trump on key initiatives as midterm election pressures intensify, with both parties skeptical of claims of an actual revolt.

headlines.headline: Iran Accuses US of Ceasefire Violations as Both Sides Exchange Strikes
headlines.summary: Iran accused the US of violating ceasefire terms after American forces shot down Iranian drones headed toward the Strait of Hormuz and intercepted ballistic missiles targeting Kuwait and Bahrain.

headlines.headline: Maine Senate Candidate Platner Faces Intimidation Allegations
headlines.summary: Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner denies reports of physical intimidation toward women as scandals shadow the Maine Democratic primary race.

headlines.headline: US Explores Redirecting Frozen Iranian Assets to Gulf Allies for War Damage Repairs
headlines.summary: The Trump administration is assessing how to steer frozen Iranian assets toward helping Gulf allies rebuild from damage inflicted during the three-month Iran-US conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Eyes Iranian Assets for Gulf Allies Reconstruction
perspectives.summary: The US government plans to use seized Iranian assets to fund reconstruction for Gulf allies affected by the US-Iran conflict, according to a source familiar with the discussions.

headlines.headline: US Envoy: Taiwan Must Spend Smarter on Defense, Learn from Ukraine and Middle East
headlines.summary: A senior US diplomat urged Taiwan to optimize defense spending and adopt drone warfare lessons from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts to maintain the military balance with China.

headlines.headline: French Telecom Consortium Signs $23.4 Billion Deal to Buy SFR from Altice
headlines.summary: Bouygues Telecom, Orange, and Free-iliad signed an agreement to acquire telecom operator SFR from Altice France in one of Europe largest telecom deals.

headlines.headline: Secret Service-Level Security as Trump to Attend NBA Finals Game 3
headlines.summary: Road closures, bag bans and extensive security measures will greet fans at Madison Square Garden as President Trump attends Game 3 of the NBA Finals between the Knicks and Spurs.

headlines.headline: North Korea Will Never Abandon Nuclear Status, Kim Yo Jong Warns
headlines.summary: Kim Jong Un's sister declared North Korea will never retreat from its nuclear-armed status, warning the country will not tolerate any threats amid ongoing tensions with the US and South Korea.

headlines.headline: US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Near Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down four Iranian attack drones launched toward the Strait of Hormuz, marking another escalation in the fragile ceasefire between Washington and Tehran.

perspectives.angle: US Strikes Iranian Radar Sites After Drone Launch Toward Strait of Hormuz
perspectives.summary: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after Iran launched four attack drones toward the Strait of Hormuz. The US military shot down all drones, calling the strike a defensive action amid fragile ceasefire negotiations.

headlines.headline: France and Allies Prepare National Sanctions to Pressure Israel Over West Bank
headlines.summary: France is coordinating with several countries to impose national sanctions on Israel over its West Bank policies, as diplomatic pressure mounts over settlement expansion and the treatment of Palestinians.

headlines.headline: Brazilian Airline Azul Cuts Flights Amid Fuel Shock From Iran Conflict
headlines.summary: Brazil's Azul airline announced further capacity cuts due to soaring jet fuel prices linked to the Iran war, as the aviation industry worldwide grapples with energy costs driven by Middle East tensions.

headlines.headline: Former French First Lady Bernadette Chirac Dies at 93
headlines.summary: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace beside President Jacques Chirac, has died. She was known for weathering political challenges and her husband's infidelities with dignity.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes St Petersburg After Putin Rejects Zelensky Meeting
headlines.summary: Ukrainian drones targeted St Petersburg after Putin rejected a proposed meeting with Zelensky. The attacks came on the final day of the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum, with one person killed and an oil depot set ablaze.

headlines.headline: India's Viral "Cockroach" Youth Movement Holds First Major Protest
headlines.summary: Hundreds joined a protest led by India's mock "Cockroach Janta Party," a youth movement with roughly 22 million Instagram followers that has become the largest online expression of dissent against the government's policies.

perspectives.angle: India Youth March Against Exam Leaks in Delhi Cockroaches Protest
perspectives.summary: Thousands of young Indians protested in New Delhi under the angry cockroaches movement, demanding accountability for exam paper leaks that have undermined educational opportunities and government job prospects.

headlines.headline: Missing US Student Found Dead in Japan
headlines.summary: The body of James Higginbotham, 20, was discovered in a mountainous area near Kyoto after he disappeared while on a family trip to Japan. Local authorities are investigating the circumstances of his death.

headlines.headline: Russia Claims Interception of 339 Ukrainian Drones Over 13 Hours
headlines.summary: Russian Defence Ministry reports its air defenses intercepted and destroyed 339 Ukrainian drones across multiple regions including Moscow in a 13-hour period, marking one of the largest drone attacks on Russian territory.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Kill 12 Including Senior Military Officers
headlines.summary: Israeli attacks in Lebanon killed 12 people including a brigadier general and captain, escalating tensions along the Israel-Lebanon border amid ongoing regional conflict.

headlines.headline: US Energy Secretary: Lower Gas Prices Require Resolution With Iran
headlines.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright stated that lowering pump prices will ultimately require a resolution with Iran to restore oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz, linking domestic energy costs to the ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Cuba's Raul Castro Makes First Public Appearance Since US Indictment
headlines.summary: Former Cuban leader Raul Castro appeared at an Interior Ministry event in Havana, his first public appearance since being indicted on murder charges in the United States last month.

headlines.headline: US Approves .5 Billion Seahawk Helicopter Sale to New Zealand
headlines.summary: The United States approved the sale of five maritime helicopters to New Zealand worth .5 billion, as Wellington moves to nearly double its military spending amid growing regional security concerns.

perspectives.angle: US Approves $1.5 Billion Seahawk Helicopter Sale to New Zealand
perspectives.summary: The US State Department approved the sale of five MH-60R Seahawk maritime helicopters to New Zealand, supporting Wellington's plans to nearly double military spending amid growing regional security concerns.

headlines.headline: Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant restores power after outage
headlines.summary: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its power supply on Saturday after an outage raised safety concerns, according to the facility's Russian-installed management.

perspectives.angle: Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Restores Power Supply After Outage
perspectives.summary: The Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine restored its Ferrosplavnaya-1 power line on Saturday, securing electricity supply to the facility amid ongoing conflict in the region.

headlines.headline: Putin signals openness to new U.S. relationship amid Ukraine stalemate
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin has indicated potential for a renewed relationship with the United States, even as negotiations to end the Ukraine war remain deadlocked and Moscow faces mounting challenges on the battlefield.

headlines.headline: Seven-month-old Palestinian baby buried after Israeli forces shooting
headlines.summary: A funeral was held for a seven-month-old Palestinian baby boy who was killed when Israeli troops fired at the vehicle he was in during a military operation in the occupied West Bank, according to medical officials.

perspectives.angle: Israeli forces kill Palestinian infant in West Bank shooting
perspectives.summary: A seven-month-old Palestinian baby was killed and his parents wounded when Israeli soldiers opened fire on their vehicle in Hebron. The Israeli military said the incident is under review and those injured were uninvolved civilians.

headlines.headline: Iranian students protest nationwide over exam changes, arrests reported
headlines.summary: Students across Iran demonstrate against changes to university entrance exams, chanting slogans demanding justice and education rights. First significant domestic protests since the US-Iran conflict began.

headlines.headline: WHO warns of nearly 500 Ebola cases in Central Africa outbreak
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization reports 100 new cases and 20 additional deaths in the Central Africa Ebola outbreak, raising alarm about the epidemic scale. Cases rose from 400 to nearly 500 in one week.

headlines.headline: Japan to designate Philippines as top priority for oil reserve support
headlines.summary: Tokyo plans to work with JICA starting this summer to provide oil reserve support to the Philippines, deepening energy cooperation amid regional tensions with China in the South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Nancy Mace breaks with Trump over Epstein vote in heated primary battle
headlines.summary: Congresswoman Nancy Mace faces consequences from Trump for her vote on Jeffrey Epstein-related legislation as she navigates a contentious primary challenge. The vote has become a flashpoint in her re-election campaign.

headlines.headline: Putin Holds Friendly Meeting With Former German Chancellor Schroeder
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin met with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder in a one-on-one meeting described as "good and friendly" by the Kremlin, amid ongoing tensions over the war in Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Putin Holds Friendly Meeting With Former German Chancellor Schröder
perspectives.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin met one-on-one with Gerhard Schröder in what the Kremlin described as a good and friendly encounter, amid ongoing tensions with the West.

headlines.headline: Senior US Diplomat Urges Taiwan to Spend Smarter on Defense
headlines.summary: Taiwan needs to "spend smarter" on defense and learn from the Ukraine and Middle East wars on using drones to counter China, the de facto U.S. ambassador to Taipei said.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan Needs to Spend Smarter on Defense, US Diplomat Says
perspectives.summary: Taiwan must "spend smarter" on defense and learn lessons from Ukraine and Middle East conflicts about drone warfare to maintain the military balance with China, the de facto US ambassador to Taipei said.

headlines.headline: Trump Faces Growing Republican Resistance in Congress
headlines.summary: Republicans and Democrats express skepticism about a revolt against Trump, but greater willingness to break with him could threaten his ambitious initiatives before the midterms.

perspectives.angle: Trump faces rare run of setbacks as courts and Congress push back
perspectives.summary: President Trump is heading into summer confronting a series of political defeats in courts, on Capitol Hill, and at the ballot box, marking an unusual stretch of resistance to his agenda.

headlines.headline: White House AI Policy Adviser Krishnan to Leave Position
headlines.summary: White House AI policy adviser Krishnan is leaving his position, according to information reports, at a time of increasing focus on artificial intelligence policy.

headlines.headline: Defense Secretary Hegseth Links Immigration to Invasion in D-Day Speech
headlines.summary: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth used a D-Day anniversary speech in France to draw parallels between WWII and current immigration challenges, calling immigration by sea an invasion that threatens nations.

headlines.headline: US Stocks Tumble After Strong Jobs Data Signals Higher Rates
headlines.summary: The S&P 500 fell more than 2.6% on Friday, its worst one-day drop of the year. An unexpectedly strong May jobs report bolstered bets that US interest rates are poised to rise, knocking tech stocks lower.

headlines.headline: 100 Days Into Iran War, Trump Struggles to Rally Domestic Support
headlines.summary: As the US-Iran conflict reaches the 100-day mark, analysts note growing war weariness among Americans. The unpopularity of the conflict may impact Republican electoral prospects and US policy decisions.

headlines.headline: Iran Launches Ballistic Missiles Toward Gulf Allies as US Intercepts
headlines.summary: Iran fired ballistic missiles toward Kuwait and Bahrain, with US forces intercepting most of them. The attack marks another escalation in the ongoing US-Iran conflict despite ceasefire talks.

perspectives.angle: Iran fires warning missiles and drones at US warships in Gulf of Oman
perspectives.summary: Iran's navy announced it fired warning missiles and drones at US warships in the Gulf of Oman, accusing the US Navy of provocative actions. This marks a significant escalation in maritime tensions amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Pakistani Interior Minister Arrives in Tehran for Iran-US Mediation Efforts
headlines.summary: Pakistan's interior minister traveled to Tehran amid ongoing diplomatic efforts to mediate between Iran and the United States as tensions continue to escalate in the Gulf region.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia Condemns Israeli Aggression Against Lebanon
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia issued a strong condemnation of what it called Israeli aggression against Lebanon, adding regional pressure amid ongoing cross-border hostilities.

headlines.headline: Albanians Protest Jared Kushner-Backed Luxury Resort on Nature Reserve
headlines.summary: Thousands of Albanians protested plans for a luxury resort backed by Jared Kushner on the country's coast, raising concerns about environmental impact on protected areas.

headlines.headline: Singapore Orders Social Media Platforms to Block Foreign Posts Targeting Indian Community
headlines.summary: Singapore's government directed social media platforms to block foreign-originated content targeting the Indian community, invoking new online harm prevention laws.

perspectives.angle: Singapore orders social media platforms to block posts targeting Indian community
perspectives.summary: Authorities directed YouTube, Facebook, and X to block 14 foreign posts that targeted the Indian community. Officials said the content undermined social cohesion.

headlines.headline: Rosneft Chief: US Energy Companies Benefit Most From Hormuz Closure
headlines.summary: Russian oil giant Rosneft's CEO Igor Sechin said US energy companies were the main beneficiaries of the Strait of Hormuz closure but warned continued tensions would undermine long-term oil demand.

perspectives.angle: Rosneft Chief: US Energy Companies Benefit from Hormuz Closure
perspectives.summary: Igor Sechin, CEO of Russian oil giant Rosneft, claims US energy companies are the main beneficiaries of the Strait of Hormuz closure amid the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Accuses China of Coordinated Operation Near Strategic South China Sea Islands
headlines.summary: A Chinese coast guard ship and survey vessel carried out what Taiwan called the first coordinated provocation near strategically located islands in the South China Sea.

perspectives.angle: China Deploys New Missiles Matching Patriot Capability Near Taiwan
perspectives.summary: State broadcaster CCTV showed troops from the Fujian-based 73rd Army Group testing new missiles in the Gobi Desert. The deployment represents a significant enhancement of Beijing's missile forces opposite Taiwan.

headlines.headline: White House AI Policy Adviser Krishnan to Leave Position
headlines.summary: White House AI policy adviser Sriram Krishnan plans to leave his position at the end of June, the Information reported on Saturday.

headlines.headline: Five Civilians Killed in Russian Attacks on Kherson Region
headlines.summary: Russian forces killed five people in three separate attacks on Ukraine's southern Kherson region on Friday, according to the regional governor, as fighting continues in the occupied territories.

perspectives.angle: Five civilians killed in Russian attacks on Ukraine's Kherson region
perspectives.summary: Russian attacks killed five people in Ukraine's southern Kherson region in three separate incidents on Friday, according to the regional governor, as fighting continues along the front lines.

headlines.headline: South Korean Protesters Demand Election Re-Run After Ballot Shortage
headlines.summary: Protesters in South Korea rallied for a second day outside a ballot-counting site, demanding a re-run of local elections held earlier this week after reports of ballot shortages disrupted voting.

headlines.headline: Israeli Strike Kills Five at Wedding in Gaza
headlines.summary: At least five people were killed and others wounded when Israeli forces bombed a wedding tent in Gaza City. The attack comes amid ongoing conflict in the region.

headlines.headline: Jamaica Experiences Island-Wide Power Blackout
headlines.summary: Jamaica is experiencing a nationwide power outage, with the energy minister confirming the blackout on social media. No cause has been given yet.

headlines.headline: Trump Urges Firing of Intelligence Community Employees
headlines.summary: President Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants the acting director of national intelligence to begin firing large numbers of employees as part of a shake-up of the US intelligence community.

perspectives.angle: Trump Urges Mass Firings at US Intelligence Agencies
perspectives.summary: President Donald Trump told the Wall Street Journal he wants acting DNI Bill Pulte to begin firing large numbers of intelligence community employees as part of a major shake-up of US spy agencies.

headlines.headline: Meta Considers Major Stock Offering for AI Infrastructure
headlines.summary: Meta is weighing raising tens of billions of dollars in a stock offering to fund AI infrastructure ambitions, according to Financial Times. The move comes as tech giants race to expand AI capabilities.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Opens Second International Airport Amid Israel-Hezbollah War
headlines.summary: Lebanon inaugurated Qlayaat airport near the Syrian border as a second international hub, converting it from military airbase to passenger use as the country seeks to shift traffic from Beirut amid the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: France Opens War Crime Probe Over Israel Flotilla Raid
perspectives.summary: French prosecutors have opened a war crimes investigation into Israel's treatment of activists aboard a Gaza-bound flotilla. Two of more than 30 French activists remain in Israeli custody.

headlines.headline: Hamas Says Cairo Talks Begin on Gaza Ceasefire Implementation
headlines.summary: Hamas announced that talks in Cairo are focusing on ending Israeli violations, opening crossings, delivering aid, and completing the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.

headlines.headline: Explosion at Myanmar building storing mining explosives kills over 45
headlines.summary: A blast at a building in northeastern Myanmar near the Chinese border killed more than 45 people and injured 70 others. The Taang National Liberation Army said gelignite stored for mining operations detonated.

headlines.headline: At least 49 migrants die of thirst in Niger desert after truck breakdown
headlines.summary: Forty-nine people died of thirst in the Sahara desert after their truck broke down while traveling from Mali to Niger for Eid al-Adha celebrations. Two survivors walked more than 50 km to alert authorities.

perspectives.angle: 49 Migrants Die of Thirst in Niger After Truck Breaks Down in Sahara Desert
perspectives.summary: Forty-nine people died of thirst when a truck broke down in a remote part of Niger's Sahara desert, highlighting the deadly risks faced by migrants crossing the region.

headlines.headline: Major fire breaks out at one of Russias biggest oil refineries
headlines.summary: A large blaze broke out at the Antipinsky Oil Refinery in Tyumen, one of Russias biggest private refineries, according to local reports.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Accuses Iran of Using Country as Bargaining Chip in US Negotiations
headlines.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun made his toughest criticism yet of Tehran, accusing Iran of leveraging Lebanon in its talks with Washington while Hezbollah wages war with Israel. The statement highlights growing Arab frustration with Iranian interference.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon President Accuses Iran of Using Country as Bargaining Chip
perspectives.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accused Iran of using Lebanon as a bargaining chip in its negotiations with the United States, in some of his toughest criticism yet of Tehran and its Lebanese ally Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: North Korea Plans 10,000-Ton Destroyer and Secret Underwater Weapons
headlines.summary: State media announced plans for a large destroyer and nuclear-armed naval fleet ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit. Kim Jong Un vowed to accelerate development of naval nuclear capabilities.

perspectives.angle: North Korea announces 10,000-ton destroyer and secret underwater weapons ahead of Xi visit
perspectives.summary: State media announced major naval expansion plans including a destroyer and underwater weapons development, just ahead of Chinese President Xi Jinping's visit to Pyongyang.

headlines.headline: Macron, Starmer, Merz to Meet Zelenskiy in London for War Talks
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, June 7, as European leaders seek to support Ukraine amid stalled peace efforts.

perspectives.angle: Macron, Starmer and Merz to meet Zelenskiy in London for Ukraine talks
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Sunday, according to the French presidency.

perspectives.angle: Macron, Starmer, Merz to Meet Zelensky in London on June 7
perspectives.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron, British Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and German Chancellor Friedrich Merz will hold talks with Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in London on Sunday. The meeting signals continued European coordination on Ukraine support.

headlines.headline: NASA Orders ISS Astronauts to Shelter During Russian Module Air Leak Repair
headlines.summary: Five astronauts aboard the International Space Station took shelter and prepared for evacuation as Russian cosmonauts attempted to fix a worsening crack in the Zvezda service module. NASA later reversed the evacuation order after repairs.

headlines.headline: Iran demands $24 billion in frozen assets as condition for US peace deal progress
headlines.summary: Iranian military adviser Mohsen Rezaei said negotiations with the US have reached a deadlock, tying progress to the release of $24 billion in frozen Iranian assets. Tensions remain high following US strikes on Iranian coastal sites.

headlines.headline: Taipei Zoo Welcomes First Red Pandas From China in Over a Decade
headlines.summary: Taiwan received red pandas from a zoo in Fujian province, marking a rare instance of animal diplomacy amid cross-strait tensions and the first such exchange since 2014.

headlines.headline: Armenia Holds Election That Could Reshape Ties With Russia and the West
headlines.summary: Armenia votes Sunday in parliamentary elections that could pivot the country away from Russia toward closer Western ties, with Prime Minister Pashinyan seeking a mandate for constitutional reforms.

perspectives.angle: Armenia Election Could Reshape Ties With Russia and the West
perspectives.summary: Armenia's parliamentary elections will determine whether the country continues pivoting toward Europe under PM Pashinyan or rekindles its traditional alliance with Moscow, in a vote seen as a test of Russian influence in the South Caucasus.

headlines.headline: Iran Fires Missiles at Bahrain and Kuwait, Escalating Regional Conflict
headlines.summary: Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones at Bahrain and Kuwait on Saturday, with Bahrain intercepting the attacks. Kuwait condemned the strikes as a dangerous escalation threatening Gulf stability.

perspectives.angle: Iran Launches Missiles at Bahrain and Kuwait After US Strikes
perspectives.summary: Iran fired missiles at US allies Bahrain and Kuwait following fresh American strikes on Iranian targets, drawing condemnation and escalating regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners Each in Latest POW Swap
headlines.summary: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel on Friday in the latest prisoner of war swap, continuing a pattern of exchanges amid the ongoing conflict.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine and Russia exchange 185 prisoners each in latest swap
perspectives.summary: Ukraine and Russia each exchanged 185 service personnel in the latest prisoner of war swap, officials in both countries said, continuing regular exchanges despite ongoing hostilities.

headlines.headline: Pope Leo arrives in Spain for week-long visit focused on migrants and abuse survivors
headlines.summary: Pope Leo arrived in Spain on Saturday for his first apostolic visit to an EU country outside Italy, where he will inaugurate a new tower at Barcelona's Sagrada Familia and meet with migrants who crossed the Atlantic, as well as survivors of clergy sexual abuse.

perspectives.angle: Pope Leo to Meet Abuse Survivors During Spain Visit
perspectives.summary: Pope Leo will meet with survivors of sexual abuse by Catholic clergy during his week-long visit to Spain, his first to an EU country outside Italy, where he will also inaugurate a new tower at Barcelona's Sagrada Familia basilica.

headlines.headline: US companies to add 40 million barrels to Strategic Petroleum Reserve after Iran war
headlines.summary: Energy Secretary Chris Wright announced that companies borrowing oil from the US Strategic Petroleum Reserve will add back an extra 40 million barrels as premiums once the Iran conflict ends, addressing concerns about depleted domestic reserves.

headlines.headline: US oil exports surge, draining Cushing inventories toward historic lows
headlines.summary: US oil exports have reached record levels as refiners worldwide scramble to fill the supply gap from Middle East war disruptions, leaving storage tanks at Cushing, Oklahoma nearly empty and raising concerns about domestic supply security.

headlines.headline: US sanctions network smuggling Iranian LPG to Asia disguised as Omani product
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department imposed sanctions on a network of individuals, entities, and tankers smuggling Iranian-origin liquid petroleum gas to South and East Asia while falsely labeling it as Omani LPG.

headlines.headline: Pentagon flags Israel as highest counterintelligence risk amid spying concerns
headlines.summary: The US Defense Intelligence Agency raised Israel's threat rating to critical, citing surveillance concerns. Officials report using burner phones and taking extra precautions during visits.

headlines.headline: Iran seizes US-linked vessel in Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran's Fars News Agency reported the seizure of a vessel belonging to a US-based shipping company in the strategic waterway, escalating maritime tensions.

headlines.headline: Ireland scrutinizes Russian-linked alumina plant as sanctions pressure mounts
headlines.summary: The Aughinish Alumina facility in Limerick faces renewed attention over its connections to Russia's military supply chain, as Europe debates sanctions enforcement.

headlines.headline: US lawmakers raise concerns over civilian nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
headlines.summary: Congress members expressed worries about uranium enrichment provisions in the proposed agreement, with experts noting it may not require adherence to nonproliferation standards.

headlines.headline: Iran World Cup Players Granted US Visas, Some Staff Still Waiting
headlines.summary: Irans World Cup soccer players received visas to enter the United States days before their first match, though some administrative staff remain without visas.

perspectives.angle: Iran Condemns US Over World Cup Visa Denials for Delegation Staff
perspectives.summary: Iran accused the US of discriminatory treatment after World Cup host nation refused visas for some delegation support staff, while players received their visas just 10 days before their opening match.

headlines.headline: Iran Reaffirms Support for Hezbollah as Broader Peace Deal Falters
headlines.summary: Iran declared continued backing for Hezbollah and demanded Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon, complicating interim negotiations between Washington and Tehran.

perspectives.angle: Iran reaffirms support for Hezbollah as broader peace deal faces complications
perspectives.summary: Iran has reaffirmed support for its Lebanese ally Hezbollah and demanded Israel withdraw from southern Lebanon, underscoring the complications facing an interim deal to end the broader conflict between the U.S. and Iran.

headlines.headline: Zelenskiy Open Letter to Putin Aimed at Wider Audience Beyond Moscow
headlines.summary: Ukraines president offered face-to-face peace talks in an open letter that analysts say was designed to resonate at the St. Petersburg investment forum and beyond.

perspectives.angle: Zelensky Appeals Directly to Putin in Open Letter Calling for Face-to-Face Peace Talks
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky published an unprecedented open letter to Vladimir Putin, proposing direct negotiations in a neutral country to end the war. The letter comes as Ukraine reports gaining more territory than it lost for the third consecutive month.

headlines.headline: US Preparing IAEA Resolution Condemning Iran
headlines.summary: The United States is preparing a draft resolution condemning Iran ahead of next week meeting of the UN nuclear watchdog Board of Governors, diplomats said, a step that could complicate wider talks between the US and Iran.

perspectives.angle: US preparing IAEA resolution condemning Iran
perspectives.summary: The United States is drafting a resolution to condemn Iran ahead of next week's IAEA Board of Governors meeting, a move that could complicate ongoing US-Iran talks as the conflict nears 100 days.

headlines.headline: Israel's Lebanon Endgame Remains Elusive Despite Ceasefire
headlines.summary: Prime Minister Netanyahu was compelled by President Trump to accept a new ceasefire with Hezbollah, but Israel's strategic objectives in Lebanon remain unclear. The agreement leaves key issues unresolved.

headlines.headline: France to Test AI-Powered Battlefield Command in NATO Exercise
headlines.summary: The French Army has developed its own large-language model called Berthier for staff officers, named after Napoleon's chief of staff. The system will be tested in June NATO exercises as militaries increasingly adopt AI for command decisions.

headlines.headline: UAE Markets Rise Despite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Dubai's stock market outperformed regional peers even as hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict faded, with investors appearing to price in limited escalation risks.

headlines.headline: Trump's 'Crazy' Rebuke Undercuts Netanyahu at Critical Moment
headlines.summary: President Trump's public criticism of Prime Minister Netanyahu over Gaza policy has exposed a rift at a crucial time, complicating Israel's position as it navigates ceasefires with both Iran and Hezbollah.

perspectives.angle: Trump's 'crazy' rebuke undercuts Netanyahu at critical moment
perspectives.summary: Trump's public criticism of Netanyahu as 'crazy' has undercut the Israeli leader at a delicate moment, highlighting tensions despite Netanyahu's portrayal of being uniquely adept at dealing with the US president.

headlines.headline: European Shares Slip as Tech Rally Stalls, Middle East Weighs
headlines.summary: European stocks ended the week lower as technology stocks paused their two-month rally and Middle East uncertainty kept investors cautious, with major indices posting weekly losses.

headlines.headline: Sea drone self-detonates near Romanian Black Sea port amid Russian jamming
headlines.summary: A sea drone self-destructed near an oil terminal in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta without causing casualties, as Ukraine said Russian jamming caused the vessel to drift off course.

headlines.headline: Russian drone attack kills four at dairy factory in Kyiv region
headlines.summary: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in the Ukrainian capital region killed four people and injured seven more, Ukrainian officials said.

perspectives.angle: Russian drone strikes kill four at Kyiv dairy factory
perspectives.summary: A Russian drone attack on a dairy factory in the Kyiv region killed four people and injured seven others, Ukrainian officials reported.

headlines.headline: Bank of Canada expected to hold rates through 2026 despite inflation risks
headlines.summary: The Bank of Canada will hold its key overnight rate at 2.25% on June 10 and for the rest of the year, according to a Reuters poll, despite rising inflation risks from conflict-driven energy prices.

perspectives.angle: ECB Expected to Hike Rates Next Week as Iran War Fuels Inflation Pressures
perspectives.summary: The European Central Bank is expected to raise interest rates, becoming the first major central bank to do so since the Iran conflict unleashed an energy crisis stoking inflationary pressures in the euro zone.

headlines.headline: UN Security Council to convene emergency meeting on Russian missile terror
headlines.summary: Ukraine requested an emergency UN Security Council meeting for June 8 in response to Russia recent large-scale ballistic missile attacks on Ukrainian cities. Foreign Minister Sybiha said Moscow continues choosing escalation over peace.

headlines.headline: Iran FM rejects Lebanese interference accusations, says focus on real foe
headlines.summary: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi dismissed Lebanese President Joseph Aoun accusation of interference, telling him to focus on the real foe as Lebanon leaders call for Iran to stop interfering in their affairs.

headlines.headline: China mobilizes SOEs and tech firms to hire record 12.7 million graduates
headlines.summary: Eight government departments launched a nationwide campaign requiring state-owned enterprises and major private tech firms to expand hiring for the class of 2026, marking one of China broadest labor market interventions in recent years.

headlines.headline: KMT leader says Taiwan can preserve peace by avoiding de jure independence
headlines.summary: Kuomintang Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun told a Harvard seminar that cross-strait peace can be maintained as long as Taipei does not cross the red line of de jure independence, advocating for both deterrence and dialogue frameworks.

headlines.headline: Putin says he sees no reason to meet Zelenskiy after Ukrainian peace overture
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin said he currently saw no reason to meet Volodymyr Zelenskiy after the Ukrainian president published an open letter proposing face-to-face talks to end the war now in its fifth year.

headlines.headline: UK house prices fall unexpectedly as Iran war impact hits market
headlines.summary: British house prices unexpectedly fell in May according to Halifax data, as higher borrowing costs and uncertainty from the Iran conflict weighed on demand.

perspectives.angle: US Job Market Rebounds Despite Iran War Costs
perspectives.summary: US employers added 172,000 jobs in May as the labor market showed resilience despite rising costs from the Iran conflict. The labor market continues to recover.

headlines.headline: Putin holds friendly meeting with former German Chancellor Schroeder
headlines.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin held a one-on-one meeting with former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder, described as "good and friendly" by Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov, amid ongoing tensions with the West.

headlines.headline: Canada reports strong job growth with unemployment rate falling to 6.6%
headlines.summary: Canada's economy added 87,800 jobs in May with unemployment falling to 6.6%, defying expectations and showing economic resilience despite signs of slower growth.

headlines.headline: Turkish fishing boat attacked in Black Sea, one sailor killed
headlines.summary: A Turkish-flagged fishing boat was attacked and sank off the northern Black Sea coast, leaving one sailor dead and four wounded. The Turkish Coast Guard is investigating the incident.

headlines.headline: OPEC Output Hits Decades-Low as US Blockade Squeezes Iran
headlines.summary: OPEC crude production fell to its lowest level in decades as the US maritime blockade of Iran disrupts exports, tightening global supply.

headlines.headline: Iran Dodges US Blockade Through Iraq as New Smuggling Route Emerges
headlines.summary: More than 50 days after the US-led maritime blockade, Iraq's Umm Qasr port has become a key smuggling route for Iran to circumvent sanctions.

headlines.headline: US and Iran Exchange Strikes in Gulf as Ceasefire Efforts Stall
headlines.summary: US forces struck Iranian coastal radar sites after shooting down drones near the Strait of Hormuz, while Iran targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain in a new escalation complicating peace efforts.

perspectives.angle: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf as ceasefire negotiations stall
perspectives.summary: US Central Command intercepted seven Iranian ballistic missiles fired at Kuwait and Bahrain, while striking Iranian coastal radar sites. The IRGC said it targeted US bases in retaliation, warning that US aggression in the region will not go unanswered.

headlines.headline: UAE Stock Markets Rally Despite Stalled US-Iran Peace Talks
headlines.summary: Dubai markets outperformed regional peers on Friday, with investors appearing to brush aside fading hopes for a diplomatic breakthrough in the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China Invests $162 Million in AI to Promote Xi Jinping Ideology
headlines.summary: Xinhuanet plans to invest over 1.1 billion yuan on an authoritative AI agent to help promote President Xi Jinping political thinking, according to Shanghai Stock Exchange filings.

perspectives.angle: China Plans $162 Million AI System to Promote Xi Jinping's Political Thinking
perspectives.summary: State-owned Xinhuanet plans to invest over 1.1 billion yuan in an "authoritative" AI agent to promote President Xi Jinping's ideology, marking a new frontier in the Communist Party's use of technology for political messaging.

headlines.headline: China's Xi Jinping to Visit North Korea in First Trip in Nearly 7 Years
headlines.summary: China announced President Xi Jinping will visit North Korea on a two-day trip from June 8, his first in nearly seven years, as Beijing seeks to reassert ties with Pyongyang, its only formal treaty ally.

perspectives.angle: Xi Jinping to visit North Korea next week in first trip since 2019
perspectives.summary: Chinese leader Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week, both countries announced Friday, in what will be his first visit since 2019. The meeting comes amid shifting regional dynamics and US-China tensions.

headlines.headline: Lebanese President Aoun Thanks Saudi Crown Prince for Support
headlines.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun spoke by phone with Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman, thanking him for Saudi support to Beirut amid ongoing regional tensions and humanitarian crisis.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Crown Prince condemns Iranian attacks on Bahrain in call with King Hamad
perspectives.summary: Mohammed bin Salman expressed solidarity with Bahrain during a phone call with King Hamad, condemning Iranian attacks and underscoring regional tensions.

headlines.headline: UNESCO Body Recommends Japan Ancient Sites for Heritage List
headlines.summary: A UNESCO advisory body has recommended adding the Asuka-Fujiwara ancient sites in Nara Prefecture to the World Heritage list, highlighting Japan historical heritage recognition.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian drone damages Romanian Black Sea port terminal
headlines.summary: A Ukrainian maritime drone self-detonated near an oil terminal in Romania's Constanta port after Russia jammed the vessel, causing it to drift off course. No casualties reported.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Maritime Drone Explodes at Romanian Black Sea Port
perspectives.summary: A Ukrainian maritime drone self-detonated near an oil terminal in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta without causing casualties. Ukraine stated Russia jammed the vessel, causing it to drift off course.

headlines.headline: Oil prices fall on mounting hopes for US-Iran de-escalation
headlines.summary: Crude prices dropped as traders gained confidence that renewed conflict between the US and Iran was becoming less likely, with peace negotiations showing signs of progress.

perspectives.angle: Oil Prices Rise on US-Iran Deal Uncertainty and Oman Terminal Suspension
perspectives.summary: Oil prices rebounded after Hezbollah rejected a Lebanon ceasefire proposal and Oman's Mina al Fahal terminal suspended oil loadings following an explosion, raising supply concerns amid stalled US-Iran peace talks.

headlines.headline: US forces board sanctioned tanker in Indian Ocean
headlines.summary: The US military conducted an interdiction of a stateless oil tanker in the Indian Ocean overnight, continuing enforcement of sanctions amid heightened regional tensions.

perspectives.angle: US Forces Board Sanctioned Tanker in Indian Ocean
perspectives.summary: U.S. forces conducted an interdiction of the stateless oil tanker Davina in the Indian Ocean, enforcing sanctions amid ongoing tensions with Iran over maritime security.

headlines.headline: Trump vows quick end to Iran war in Wisconsin campaign stop
headlines.summary: President Trump promised to swiftly end the Iran conflict and remove a source of high prices while campaigning for Republicans in rural Wisconsin ahead of midterm elections.

perspectives.angle: US House Votes to Limit Trump's Iran War Powers
perspectives.summary: The House passed a war powers resolution 215-208, with four Republicans joining Democrats in a rare rebuke of President Trump's handling of the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: SpaceX IPO draws $150 billion in demand, twice the offering size
headlines.summary: Investor demand for SpaceX's $75 billion IPO has reached approximately $150 billion, making it roughly two times oversubscribed ahead of the highly anticipated market debut.

headlines.headline: SpaceX locks in Google cloud deal ahead of IPO
headlines.summary: SpaceX signed a multi-year cloud services agreement with Google for computing capacity, securing additional revenue streams ahead of its stock market debut next week.

headlines.headline: US Approves $1.98 Billion Counter-Drone System Sale to Kuwait
headlines.summary: The US State Department approved a major sale of counter-unmanned aerial systems to Kuwait as Gulf states ramp up air defenses amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: UN Security Council Demands Release of Houthi-Detained Staff After Two Years
headlines.summary: Council members renewed calls for the release of UN and NGO personnel held by Yemen Houthi rebels for two years, warning that detentions worsen humanitarian conditions.

headlines.headline: Most Asia-Pacific Firms Using AI Without Job Cuts, Survey Finds
headlines.summary: About 74% of companies surveyed across the Asia-Pacific region have deployed or piloted AI programs while maintaining workforce levels.

headlines.headline: South Korean Defense Stocks Surge on Middle East Demand
headlines.summary: Defense stocks in South Korea are climbing as Middle Eastern countries seek faster deliveries of Korean-made missile defense systems.

headlines.headline: Zelenskyy calls for direct negotiations with Putin in neutral country
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy wrote a public letter to Putin proposing face-to-face talks hosted by a neutral third country, suggesting Switzerland, Turkey or Arab states. He proposed a full ceasefire during negotiations and an all-for-all prisoner exchange as a first step.

headlines.headline: Senate passes  billion immigration enforcement bill after all-night session
headlines.summary: The Senate approved funding for ICE and Border Patrol through the end of Trump's term after Republicans defeated multiple attempts to ban Trump's controversial .776 billion settlement fund for allies claiming political persecution.

headlines.headline: Half of Latin Americans deported from US to Congo have returned home
headlines.summary: More than half of the 15 Latin Americans deported to Congo under Trump's third-country deportation policy have returned to their countries of origin, even though US judges ruled they likely face persecution at home.

headlines.headline: US House Defies Trump, Passes Ukraine Aid Package and Russia Sanctions
headlines.summary: The US House approved legislation supporting Ukraine with direct loans and assistance while imposing new sanctions on Russia, marking a significant rebuke of President Trump's foreign policy stance.

perspectives.angle: US House Passes Russia Sanctions and Ukraine Aid Bill
perspectives.summary: The House voted 226-195 for the Ukraine Support Act, with Republicans joining Democrats to force the vote. The bill had languished for months before reaching the floor.

headlines.headline: China Tracks Dutch Frigate Through Taiwan Strait
headlines.summary: China's military dispatched naval and air assets to monitor the Dutch frigate De Ruyter during its transit through the Taiwan Strait, highlighting continued tensions in the region.

perspectives.angle: Chinese Military Tracks Dutch Frigate Transiting Taiwan Strait
perspectives.summary: Chinas military dispatched naval and air assets to monitor a Dutch warship passing through the Taiwan Strait, highlighting continued tensions over international waterways in the region.

headlines.headline: Irish PM Bans Israeli Ministers Over Flotilla Comments
headlines.summary: Ireland banned Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich and National Security Minister Itamar Ben Gvir for anti-Palestinian remarks and actions related to the Gaza flotilla, calling their behavior a push for the "elimination of Palestinians from Palestine."

headlines.headline: Slovenia Removes Palestinian Flag in Sharp Pro-Israel Shift
headlines.summary: Slovenia's new prime minister removed the Palestinian flag from government buildings in a symbolic break from the previous administration's stance, following Slovenia's earlier condemnation of Israel over Gaza.

headlines.headline: Russian Budget Deficit Doubles to $81 Billion Amid War Strain
headlines.summary: Russia's federal budget deficit reached 6 trillion rubles ($81.4 billion) in the first five months of 2026, doubling from the same period last year as war costs continue to mount.

headlines.headline: ISS Astronauts Briefly Ordered to Prepare for Evacuation
headlines.summary: NASA ordered five astronauts to shelter in docked SpaceX Dragon spacecraft for two hours as a Russian crew attempted to fix a worsening air leak in the Zvezda service module. Operations have since returned to normal.

perspectives.angle: ISS astronauts briefly take shelter during leak repair on Russian side
perspectives.summary: NASA ordered astronauts to shelter in a SpaceX capsule while Russian cosmonauts worked to fix a fresh leak aboard the International Space Station. The crew returned to normal operations after repairs were paused.

headlines.headline: SpaceX IPO Bars China and Hong Kong Investors
headlines.summary: SpaceX underwriters have barred investors in China and Hong Kong from participating in the IPO, with the company seeking to raise $75 billion in what could be one of the largest public offerings ever.

headlines.headline: US House Approves Ukraine Aid and Russia Sanctions
headlines.summary: The House passed the Ukraine Support Act 226-195 after a discharge petition forced the vote, defying President Trump and Senate Republicans. A handful of Republicans joined Democrats in supporting the legislation.

headlines.headline: Somalia Government Restores Order After Capital Fighting
headlines.summary: Somali security forces restored order in two districts of Mogadishu after government troops and militias allied with opposition politicians clashed, forcing some civilians to flee.

perspectives.angle: Somalia Restores Order After Capital Fighting Between Government and Opposition Militias
perspectives.summary: Somali security forces restored order in two districts of Mogadishu after government troops and opposition-allied militias exchanged fire, forcing civilians to flee the capital.

headlines.headline: Russia and Uzbekistan Begin Construction of First Nuclear Power Plant in Central Asia
headlines.summary: The Kremlin announced the start of construction on a Russian-built nuclear power plant in Uzbekistan, the first in post-Soviet Central Asia, aimed at meeting growing regional energy demands.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Destroys Russian Drone Base at Occupied Donetsk Airport
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces conducted an operation to degrade Russia's military capabilities at the occupied Donetsk Airport, destroying a drone base. The operation continues Ukraine's strategy of targeting Russian military infrastructure.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine releases footage of drones striking Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base near St. Petersburg
perspectives.summary: Ukraine's Unmanned Systems Forces published video of drones attacking a Russian warship armed with guided missiles at the Kronstadt naval base, demonstrating Kyiv's reach into Russia's Baltic waters.

headlines.headline: Armenia Faces Pivotal Election on Future Away From Russia
headlines.summary: Armenia's parliamentary election on June 7 will test whether the small democracy can chart its own future and how much the country will pivot away from Russian influence toward the West.

headlines.headline: Indonesia Volcano Eruption Forces Airport Closure
headlines.summary: Mount Lewotobi Laki-Laki in Indonesia erupted, forcing a local airport to close. The volcano is under Indonesia's second-highest alert level with a 5km exclusion zone.

headlines.headline: Swedish Court Rules Seized Cargo Ship Can Be Handed to Ukraine
headlines.summary: A Swedish court ruled that a cargo vessel seized in the Baltic Sea can be handed over to Ukraine. The ship is suspected of illegally transporting grain from Russian-occupied territory.

perspectives.angle: Swedish Court Rules Seized Cargo Ship Can Go to Ukraine
perspectives.summary: A Swedish court ruled that a cargo vessel seized in the Baltic Sea can be handed to Ukraine. The ship is suspected of illegally transporting grain from Russian-occupied territory.

headlines.headline: US Judge Strikes Down Trump Immigration Policy on 39 Countries
headlines.summary: A federal judge invalidated Trump administration policies that made it harder for immigrants from dozens of countries to receive decisions, ruling the measures unlawful after the shooting of two National Guard members.

perspectives.angle: US federal judge strikes down Trump immigration policy affecting 39 countries
perspectives.summary: A federal judge in Boston struck down a Trump administration policy that made it harder for immigrants from 39 African, Asian, Latin American and Middle Eastern countries to stay in the US. The ruling said the policy threw immigrants into "indeterminate legal limbo."

headlines.headline: Russian Satellites Jamming GPS Across Europe
headlines.summary: Scientists and U.S. military briefers have linked widespread GPS interference incidents across Europe to Russian satellites, revealing coordinated electronic warfare activity targeting European infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Taiwan-China Coast Guard Standoff Near Pratas Islands
headlines.summary: Taiwanese and Chinese Coast Guards engaged in a tense standoff near the strategically located Pratas Islands in the South China Sea, the second such incident in two weeks amid escalating maritime tensions.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan and China Coast Guards Clash Near Pratas Islands in South China Sea
perspectives.summary: Taiwan reported a second tense standoff with Chinese coast guard vessels near the strategically located Pratas Islands in two weeks, highlighting ongoing tensions in the northern South China Sea.

headlines.headline: Turkey Targets Defense Sales Surge as West Rearms
headlines.summary: Two decades of state investment have transformed Turkey into a major drone and military equipment exporter, and the NATO member is now seeking to capitalize on Western rearmament and shifting security alliances.

perspectives.angle: Turkey Expands Defense Exports as NATO Rearms and Alliances Shift
perspectives.summary: Two decades of state investment have transformed Turkey into a major drone and military equipment exporter, with the NATO member now looking to capitalize on Western rearmament efforts and changing security partnerships.

headlines.headline: US Imposes New Iran-Related Sanctions Targeting Entities, Individuals and Tankers
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department announced new sanctions targeting Iranian entities, individuals, and LPG tankers as tensions remain high amid stalled negotiations.

headlines.headline: Twelve Democrats Vote Against House Armed Services Committee NDAA
headlines.summary: A dozen Democrats opposed the HASC nearly $1.15 trillion version of the annual defense policy bill, marking a rare committee split.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong shares of HSBC, Standard Chartered fall on China capital controls
headlines.summary: Hong Kong-listed shares of HSBC, Standard Chartered and AIA fell sharply on concerns Beijing tighter capital controls could dent business of global financial firms with mainland China exposure.

perspectives.angle: SpaceX IPO bars investors from China and Hong Kong
perspectives.summary: SpaceX underwriters have barred investors in China and Hong Kong from participating in the planned IPO, Bloomberg reported, in a move reflecting ongoing US-China tensions.

headlines.headline: US officials discuss government stakes in AI companies
headlines.summary: Senior US officials held preliminary discussions with major AI companies about the potential for government to buy shares in their firms, NOTUS reported, reflecting growing national security concerns around AI development.

headlines.headline: Azerbaijani Crew Members Killed in Ukraine Drone Attack on Cargo Ships
headlines.summary: Russia accused Ukraine of killing Azerbaijani crew members aboard two cargo vessels headed for a Russian port in a drone attack in the Sea of Azov. Baku confirmed at least five of its citizens were killed in the strikes.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine launches unprecedented drone attack on St Petersburg during economic forum
perspectives.summary: Russia says it shot down 376 Ukrainian drones overnight, including 86 over the Leningrad region. Zelenskyy said Ukrainian drones struck naval arsenals and a base in Kronstadt, reaching about 1,000 km into Russian territory.

headlines.headline: WFP Warns Middle East Conflict Pushing Millions Toward Hunger
headlines.summary: The UN World Food Programme warned that the Middle East conflict is driving millions closer to hunger, with rising fuel and transport costs inflating food prices while aid agencies face funding shortfalls.

perspectives.angle: Middle East Conflict Pushing Millions Toward Hunger as Food Prices Soar, UN Warns
perspectives.summary: The World Food Programme reports that the Middle East conflict is driving millions toward hunger, with rising fuel and transport costs inflating food prices while funding shortfalls force aid agencies to scale back operations.

headlines.headline: China Tracks Dutch Warship Transiting Taiwan Strait
headlines.summary: China's military dispatched naval and air assets to track and monitor the Dutch frigate HNLMS De Ruyter as it transited the Taiwan Strait, highlighting continued tensions in the strategically vital waterway.

headlines.headline: Former Tibet Government Head Sentenced to Life for Corruption
headlines.summary: Che Dalha, the former head of the Tibet Autonomous Region government, was sentenced to life in prison for corruption in a high-profile crackdown on graft, China's official Xinhua news agency reported.

headlines.headline: Finland blocks property purchases linked to Chinese network over security concerns
headlines.summary: Finnish authorities halted property acquisitions after determining sites were located in strategically sensitive areas.

headlines.headline: Bulgaria PM warns Europe cannot defeat nuclear-armed Russia, calls for diplomacy
headlines.summary: Rumen Radev told EU-Western Balkans summit that Europe cannot achieve conventional victory against the worlds largest nuclear power.

headlines.headline: Associate of Manchester synagogue attacker admits separate terror plot
headlines.summary: An Islamic extremist pleaded guilty to planning an attack on the UK Defence Academy with the man who later killed two people at a Manchester synagogue.

headlines.headline: Over 1,000 Nigerians Seek Repatriation from South Africa After Xenophobic Attacks
headlines.summary: At least 1,094 Nigerians have registered for voluntary return from South Africa following xenophobic attacks, a sharp increase from 130 previously seeking repatriation, Nigeria's government said.

perspectives.angle: Mozambique Says Five Citizens Killed in Xenophobic Attacks in South Africa
perspectives.summary: Mozambique reported that five of its citizens were killed in xenophobic attacks in South Africa, highlighting ongoing regional tensions over migration and violence.

headlines.headline: Israeli Supreme Court Overturns Ban on Red Cross Prison Visits
headlines.summary: Israel's Supreme Court ruled that authorities must allow the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit Palestinian prisoners, overturning a ban implemented at the start of the Gaza war.

perspectives.angle: Haredi Protesters Storm Home of Israeli Supreme Court Judge Over IDF Draft
perspectives.summary: Ultra-Orthodox demonstrators breached the home of a senior Israeli judge amid protests against arrests of yeshiva students who refuse military service, highlighting deep social divisions in Israel.

headlines.headline: White House National Security Council Europe Chief Departs in Staff Shake-Up
headlines.summary: The White House's top Europe expert is leaving as part of a broader National Security Council reorganization that sources say could give Secretary of State Marco Rubio tighter control over day-to-day policy making.

headlines.headline: Azerbaijan denies CNN report of Israeli operations against Iran from its territory
headlines.summary: Azerbaijan rejected CNN claims that Israel was conducting operations against Iran from Azerbaijani territory, dismissing the allegations as baseless. Baku reaffirmed its commitment to regional stability and good-neighborly relations.

headlines.headline: EU promises targeted Schengen visa restrictions for Russians after backlash
headlines.summary: The European Union vowed to impose targeted restrictions on Schengen visas for Russian citizens after 11 European countries, led by Sweden, criticized the fragmentation of visa policies. The move addresses growing pressure to tighten entry rules.

headlines.headline: Turkish Inflation Exceeds Expectations Amid Regional Conflict
headlines.summary: Turkish consumer prices rose 1.7% monthly and 32.6% annually in May, slightly above forecasts. The data highlights economic fallout from the US-Iran conflict and raises questions about monetary policy direction.

headlines.headline: Senate Blocks FISA Surveillance Law Reauthorization
headlines.summary: Seven Republicans joined Democrats to block debate on reauthorizing the expiring foreign surveillance law, dealing a blow to the program days before it "goes dark." The vote complicates efforts to extend Section 702 authorities.

headlines.headline: Lebanon Ceasefire Hopes Dim as Fighting Continues
headlines.summary: The US-brokered ceasefire agreement requires Hezbollah to stop firing first, but the Iran-backed group was not party to the talks and has rejected the deal. Fighting continues as diplomatic efforts face growing obstacles.

headlines.headline: Azerbaijani citizens among dead in Sea of Azov ship attacks
headlines.summary: Five Azerbaijani citizens were killed and three injured in attacks on two cargo vessels in the Russian-occupied Sea of Azov, raising questions about the scope of Ukrainian drone operations in the strategic waterway.

headlines.headline: Japan and Indonesia begin talks on destroyer export
headlines.summary: Japan's Defense Ministry announced an agreement with Indonesia to begin discussions on exporting Asagiri-class destroyers, marking a significant step in Japan's post-war defense export expansion.

headlines.headline: Senate Democrats block spy powers extension over DNI nominee
headlines.summary: Senate Democrats blocked a motion to begin debate on extending enhanced surveillance powers to protest President Trump's choice of Pulte for DNI, raising questions about the renewal of key intelligence authorities.

headlines.headline: Nationalism gains ground in Japan with anti-foreign rhetoric
headlines.summary: Japan is moving in a more nationalist and conservative direction, fueled by growing anti-foreign rhetoric that marks a significant shift in the country's political discourse.

headlines.headline: UN Doubles Lebanon Aid Appeal as War Drives Humanitarian Surge
headlines.summary: The United Nations doubled its funding appeal for Lebanon as the Middle East conflict enters its fourth month, warning of surging humanitarian needs from rising costs and displacement.

headlines.headline: Ebola Burial Team Attacked in Congo as Outbreak Spreads
headlines.summary: Residents attacked an Ebola burial team in South Kivu province, forcing responders to abandon a coffin and raising fears of further transmission as Congo battles a growing outbreak.

headlines.headline: Sweden's Gripen Fighter Jets Face Combat Debut as Ukraine Names Them Backbone of Air Force
headlines.summary: Ukraine has designated the Swedish Gripen as the backbone of its future air force, marking the first time the jet will be tested in its designed role: confronting Russian forces. The decision comes as Ukraine restructures its military capabilities.

headlines.headline: Aselsan Unveils Ship-Killing Kiliç Kamikaze Underwater Drones at Efes 2026
headlines.summary: Turkish defense company Aselsan showcased its new Kiliç (Sword) autonomous underwater drones designed to target enemy vessels, demonstrated to international observers at the Efes military exercises.

headlines.headline: China Pushes Back Against OECD Report Detailing Outsized Industrial Subsidies
headlines.summary: Beijing is contesting an Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development report that highlighted China's massive industrial subsidies, pulling the country into a public debate over state support for its economy.

headlines.headline: Legal Experts Accuse Palestine Action Judge of Bias, Demand Recusal
headlines.summary: A complaint signed by over 3,000 people accuses Justice Johnson of personal animosity toward defendants in a Palestine Action case, raising concerns about judicial impartiality.

headlines.headline: South Korea urges tech giants to share AI windfall with workers
headlines.summary: Labor Minister Lee Jung-sik called on major tech companies to distribute excess AI profits to suppliers and employees, warning of worsening inequality risks from the AI boom.

headlines.headline: Armenia election tests Pashinyan's Western pivot after Azerbaijan war
headlines.summary: Parliamentary elections on June 7 will determine whether Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan can continue Armenia's shift away from Russia toward closer ties with the EU and US.

headlines.headline: Putin Envoy: Russia-US Economic Talks Continue Despite Ukraine Pause
headlines.summary: President Putin's special envoy Kirill Dmitriev said Russia-U.S. talks on economic and energy issues are continuing despite a pause in Ukraine peace negotiations, revealing sustained back-channel dialogue between Moscow and Washington.

perspectives.angle: Russia-US Energy and Economy Talks Continue Despite Ukraine Pause
perspectives.summary: Putin envoy Kirill Dmitriev confirmed ongoing economic and energy discussions with US counterparts, even as Ukraine peace negotiations remain stalled.

headlines.headline: UN Peacekeeper Killed in Lebanon Shelling
headlines.summary: A Serbian U.N. peacekeeper in Lebanon died from wounds sustained when mortar shells hit his position near Marjayoun in southeastern Lebanon, marking the seventh peacekeeper killed in the country since March.

perspectives.angle: UN peacekeeper killed in Lebanon shelling amid escalating conflict
perspectives.summary: A Serbian UN peacekeeper died from wounds sustained in mortar shelling near Marjayoun, the seventh peacekeeper killed in Lebanon since March.

headlines.headline: Lebanese President: Ceasefire Could Take Effect Within 24 Hours
headlines.summary: Lebanese President Joseph Aoun said a U.S.-brokered ceasefire agreed between Lebanon and Israel could come into force within 24 hours of all concerned parties approving it, as diplomatic efforts continue.

headlines.headline: Japanese Yen Tests 160 Level as Officials Push Back
headlines.summary: The Japanese yen tested the key 160 barrier against the dollar for a third session, drawing sharp pushback from officials, while Middle East tensions fueled safe-haven flows into the U.S. currency.

headlines.headline: Myanmar Junta Turns to Russia for Cyber Capabilities
headlines.summary: Myanmar military seeks Russian cooperation to strengthen cyber operations, with Jane analysis suggesting the partnership aims to bolster the junta digital capabilities amid ongoing conflict.

headlines.headline: Brunei Appoints Prince Abdul Mateen as Foreign Minister
headlines.summary: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah names his helicopter-flying son as foreign minister in a cabinet reshuffle seen as preparing the next generation of leaders amid concerns about the sultan health.

headlines.headline: China Tightens Control Over Tech Sector to Counter West
headlines.summary: Beijing increases restrictions on Chinese technology executives as Xi Jinping seeks to maintain technological advantage against Western competition and prevent talent drain.

headlines.headline: Global Tech Stocks Plunge Amid AI Bubble Fears
headlines.summary: Technology shares fall sharply worldwide over concerns that AI company valuations have surged too high, triggering broad market sell-off across major exchanges.

headlines.headline: Mali Junta Offers $3.5 Million Reward for Sahel al-Qaeda Leader
headlines.summary: Military government offers bounty for information leading to arrest or killing of Iyad Ag Ghaly, head of al-Qaeda Sahel branch, as it battles insurgencies across the region.

headlines.headline: US imposes sanctions on Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel
headlines.summary: The US Treasury Department sanctioned Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel, his wife, and other individuals affiliated with the Cuban government in the latest move to pressure the islands leadership.

perspectives.angle: US Sanctions Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel
perspectives.summary: The United States imposed sanctions on Cuban President Miguel Díaz-Canel and four other individuals in the latest move to pressure the island's leadership. The measures come amid ongoing tensions between Washington and Havana.

headlines.headline: Oman suspends oil loading at Mina al Fahal after explosion
headlines.summary: Omans key Mina al Fahal oil export terminal suspended loading operations following an explosion near its mooring berths, with reports suggesting a suspected drone attack. Operations have since resumed.

headlines.headline: Germany and France propose faster EU benefits for Balkan aspirants
headlines.summary: Germany and France proposed giving aspiring EU members from the Balkans and Moldova greater access to EU programs and the single market before formal membership, according to a discussion paper ahead of the Montenegro summit.

headlines.headline: EU and Balkan Leaders Meet in Montenegro as Enlargement Gains Urgency
headlines.summary: European Union and Balkan leaders convened in Montenegro for talks on expanding the bloc, underscoring the strategic importance of the region for EU integration.

perspectives.angle: EU Leaders Push Faster Expansion at Balkan Summit in Montenegro
perspectives.summary: European leaders used a summit in Montenegro to accelerate EU membership prospects for Balkan nations as enlargement gains strategic urgency.

headlines.headline: Bank of Japan Set to Raise Rates in June Unless Middle East Escalation Disrupts Markets
headlines.summary: The BOJ is expected to raise interest rates this month unless the Middle East conflict sharply escalates, as rising fuel costs add to price pressures in the economy.

perspectives.angle: Bank of Japan Expected to Raise Rates in June
perspectives.summary: The BOJ is expected to raise interest rates this month unless a sharp escalation in the Middle East conflict upends markets. Rising fuel costs from the energy shock are adding to inflation pressure.

headlines.headline: UK Construction Activity Shrinks at Fastest Pace in Six Years on Iran War Uncertainty
headlines.summary: Britain's construction sector contracted at the sharpest pace since 2020 as economic uncertainty and inflation from the Iran conflict triggered a steep decline in new work.

headlines.headline: Brunei Sultan Appoints Sons as Ministers in Major Cabinet Reshuffle
headlines.summary: Brunei's ruler announced a significant cabinet reshuffle, appointing two younger sons as ministers in a sign of potential succession planning in the oil-rich sultanate.

perspectives.angle: Brunei Sultan appoints sons as ministers in major cabinet reshuffle
perspectives.summary: Sultan Hassanal Bolkiah announced a significant cabinet shake-up, creating new portfolios and appointing two of his younger sons as ministers in a move seen as preparing for succession.

headlines.headline: Ukraine Weighs Ending 'Hot Phase' of War Before Winter
headlines.summary: Kyiv officials see a realistic opportunity to end active fighting before winter 2026, citing stalled Russian offensives and expanding Ukrainian long-range strikes, though air defense shortages remain a concern.

headlines.headline: UK Military Chief Warns of "Most Dangerous Period" Amid Russian Incursions
headlines.summary: Chief of the Defence Staff Sir Richard Knighton told the BBC that Russian incursions into UK defenses risk crossing a line, describing the current security environment as the most dangerous period he has known.

perspectives.angle: Germany Vows to Accelerate Military Buildup Amid Russian Threat
perspectives.summary: German Defense Minister pledges to speed up military procurement and strengthen the Bundeswehr deterrence capabilities in response to the ongoing Russian threat.

perspectives.angle: Germany Vows to Accelerate Military Buildup Citing Russian Threat
perspectives.summary: Germany pledged to speed up its military expansion efforts, citing ongoing security concerns related to Russia as the driving factor behind the accelerated defense spending.

headlines.headline: Taiwan Forges Thicker Silicon Shield as AI Demand Boosts Defense Spending
headlines.summary: As Nvidia and Intel leaders praise Taiwan's role in global supply chains, the island's importance grows with AI moving from cloud to local devices. With GDP set to expand 10%, Taipei can boost defense spending amid rising tensions.

headlines.headline: Seven detained as Hong Kong police tighten security at Tiananmen vigil site
headlines.summary: Hong Kong police detained seven people near the former site of the annual Tiananmen vigil as authorities ramped up patrols ahead of the June 4 anniversary. The vigil has been banned since 2020.

perspectives.angle: Hong Kong Holds Patriotic Carnival on Former Tiananmen Vigil Site
perspectives.summary: For the fourth consecutive year, a patriotic food carnival replaced the traditional candlelit vigil in Victoria Park on the 37th anniversary of the Tiananmen crackdown, requiring security checks for visitors.

headlines.headline: Japan foreign reserves plunge  billion after currency interventions
headlines.summary: Japans foreign reserves, the worlds second largest after China, fell by  billion in May following intervention to support the yen. The data reveals the scale of Tokyos currency defense efforts.

headlines.headline: Hong Kong MTR raises €3 billion in first euro green bond sale
headlines.summary: Hong Kongs rail operator MTR Corporation has raised €3 billion in its first public euro-denominated green bond sale, signaling continued access to international capital markets amid regional uncertainty.

headlines.headline: John Bolton to Plead Guilty in Classified Documents Case
headlines.summary: Former Trump national security adviser John Bolton will plead guilty to mishandling classified documents, facing a $2.25 million fine as part of a plea deal with prosecutors.

perspectives.angle: Former Trump adviser John Bolton to plead guilty in classified documents case
perspectives.summary: John Bolton, who served as Trump's national security adviser and became a prominent critic, plans to plead guilty to mishandling classified documents and will pay a $2.25 million fine as part of a plea deal with federal prosecutors.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Support Plunges in Northern Israel Over Lebanon Stance
headlines.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu faces plunging support in northern Israel where Hezbollah rocket fire has been heaviest, putting pressure on him to take a more hawkish stance before elections.

perspectives.angle: Netanyahu Faces Plummeting Support in Northern Israel as Voters Demand Tougher Lebanon Stance
perspectives.summary: A new poll shows Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu losing support in the electorally critical north where Hezbollah rocket fire has been heaviest, increasing pressure for a more aggressive approach.

headlines.headline: Australia Investigates KPMG Partners Over Audit Leak Scandal
headlines.summary: Australia corporate regulator launched a formal investigation into three KPMG partners over whistleblower allegations of misusing confidential client data to win audit contracts.

headlines.headline: IAEA Report Shows Little Change in Iran Nuclear Program Despite War
headlines.summary: The UN nuclear watchdog sent a report to member states with no major changes to its assessment of Iran's nuclear program, despite three months of US-Israeli conflict aimed at preventing Tehran from building an atomic bomb.

headlines.headline: UN nuclear watchdog raises proliferation concerns over Iran sites
headlines.summary: The IAEA expressed concern in a confidential report about lack of access to verify nuclear material at Iranian sites, raising proliferation fears amid ongoing US-Iran tensions.

headlines.headline: US and Europeans fail to agree on new Bosnia peace deal diplomat
headlines.summary: Washington and European partners could not reach consensus on appointing a new diplomat to oversee the 1995 Dayton peace agreement, highlighting transatlantic diplomatic friction.

headlines.headline: Russia flooding Ukraine with fake migrant stories, disinformation campaign exposed
headlines.summary: A Russian disinformation campaign is spreading false claims about a migrant influx in Ukraine, exploiting social divisions as part of hybrid warfare tactics.

headlines.headline: French jets scrambled 11 times in a week to intercept Russian aircraft over Baltics
headlines.summary: French warplanes were scrambled 11 times in the past week as part of NATOs Baltic Air Policing Mission, with Frances armed forces describing the incursions as a higher-than-usual number of provocations by Russia.

perspectives.angle: French jets scramble 11 times in a week to intercept Russian aircraft over Baltics
perspectives.summary: French warplanes were scrambled 11 times as part of NATO's Baltic Air Policing Mission, with France's armed forces spokesperson describing a higher-than-usual number of Russian "provocations" in the region.

headlines.headline: US imposes sanctions on Cuban President Diaz-Canel and family members
headlines.summary: The United States has imposed sanctions on Cuban President Miguel Diaz-Canel, his wife and several senior figures, escalating pressure on the communist regime amid Cubas ongoing economic and energy crisis.

headlines.headline: US Journalist Pleads Guilty to Acting as Illegal Chinese Agent
headlines.summary: An American journalist pleaded guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government in a case that highlights Beijing's efforts to infiltrate US media organizations. The Justice Department announced the plea as tensions between Washington and Beijing continue.

headlines.headline: China Releases Satellite Images of Nvidia and Apple HQs
headlines.summary: A Chinese satellite company published bird's-eye photos of Nvidia and Apple headquarters, showcasing routine satellite news capabilities despite being under sanctions.

headlines.headline: China Demands End to US Cuba Blockade, Rejects Terrorism Charges
headlines.summary: China's foreign ministry rejected US claims that Cuba sponsors terrorism and called for an end to sanctions on the island nation.

headlines.headline: Taiwan President Lai Commemorates Tiananmen on 37th Anniversary
headlines.summary: President William Lai urged China to face up to history on the 37th anniversary of the Tiananmen Square crackdown.

headlines.headline: Taiwan to Expand Anti-Ship Missile Arsenal to Counter Chinese Threat
headlines.summary: Taiwan plans to increase its anti-ship missile arsenal to over 1,800 by early 2029 to enhance defenses against potential blockade or invasion from China.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan to Expand Anti-Ship Missile Arsenal to Counter China Threat
perspectives.summary: Taiwan plans to increase its anti-ship missile arsenal to over 1,800 by early 2029, enhancing capabilities to counter potential blockade or invasion threats from China.

headlines.headline: Trump Ceasefires Fail to Stop Violence Across Middle East
headlines.summary: Residents in Gaza, southern Lebanon, northern Israel, and Kuwait faced ongoing attacks this week despite multiple US-arranged ceasefires supposedly in effect.

perspectives.angle: Trump-arranged ceasefires failing to stop violence across Middle East
perspectives.summary: Residents of Gaza, south Lebanon, northern Israel and Kuwait all came under fire this week despite U.S.-arranged ceasefires supposedly in force, highlighting the fragility of American peace efforts in the region.

headlines.headline: Putin Faces Competing Visions of War and Peace at Russia Investment Forum
headlines.summary: At Russia's premier annual investment conference in St. Petersburg, Vladimir Putin confronted rival outlines of Russia's future as the Ukraine war continues.

headlines.headline: Gold rises as Middle East ceasefire hopes pressure dollar
headlines.summary: Gold prices gained more than 1% as oil prices slipped on optimism over a potential end to the Iran conflict, which pressured the dollar and caused bond yields to fall.

perspectives.angle: Global supply chains under pressure from Middle East war, New York Fed reports
perspectives.summary: Federal Reserve data shows elevated global supply chain pressure in May due to the Middle East conflict, signaling inflation pressures will remain formidable for the foreseeable future.

headlines.headline: Severe El Nino threatens Asian crops as dry weather disrupts planting
headlines.summary: Dry weather is disrupting crop planting across Asia, raising concerns about food supplies, as an expected severe El Nino weather pattern threatens more damage.

headlines.headline: US explores major expansion of Lebanese army support as key to Hezbollah disarmament
headlines.summary: The United States is considering significantly expanding support for the Lebanese Armed Forces as part of a broader strategy to facilitate Hezbollah disarmament, marking a potential shift in US approach to the Lebanon conflict.
